title: orchis elata subsp. ambigua
intro: Orchis elata subsp. ambigua, also known as the tall orchid or mountain orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective against infections and wounds. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
